discogs.name: David Magdziarz
discogs.profile:
[b]David Magdziarz[/b] (b. July 1958) is an American art photographer, columnist, and editor from Chicago. He is best known for collaborating with the cult underground band [b][a=ONO][/b] in the early 1980s and was a long-standing contributor and editorial staff member of [i][b]UPTOWN[/b][/i], a Swedish-based fanzine dedicated to [a=Prince] and named after his early-1980s eponymous [url=/label/305982]home studio[/url]. Magdziarz worked with the publication for eleven years, first contributing to Issue №9 in July 1993 and joining the staff on №14 in July/September 1994. He served as [i]Uptown[/i]'s Contributing Editor between Issues №21 in October 1995 and the final №60 in March 2004. 

Magdziarz was extensively involved in a legal standoff between [i]UPTOWN[/i] and the artist that ensued in late February 1999 after [url=/label/441405]Paisley Park Enterprises[/url] filed a copyright infringement lawsuit on behalf of [a=Prince Rogers Nelson] against nine fan websites and two periodical
